prompt: A StudioOps Offline Scheduling & Community system with main features including appointment booking for photography sessions, client messaging, interactive community threads, configurable health declarations, in-app notifications, and back-office search. The primary roles are Administrator (studio owner/manager), Photographer (service provider), Client (customer), and Moderator (community governance). The Vue.js single-page interface uses frontend routing to separate Booking, Messages, Community, Forms, and Admin views, with responsive layouts for phone and desktop. Clients browse configurable service items (e.g., “Headshots – 30 min – $175.00” and “Family Session – 90 min – $450.00”), pick time slots from a photographer availability calendar, and submit a booking that moves through pending/confirmed/rescheduled/canceled/completed; the UI enforces conflict detection by auto-locking a selected slot for 10 minutes in a booking Drawer and releasing it on timeout or cancellation. Messaging provides in-app order-status notifications and a live status timeline (accepted/arrived/started/ended) shown as a stepper component; notification preferences are configurable per user (in-app always available; “email/SMS” are displayed as extensible channels but remain disabled in offline mode). The health declaration form engine lets Admins build templates with conditional show/hide and required rules, rendered to clients as a step-by-step wizard with draft save, submit confirmation Modal, and duplicate-submit prevention that blocks re-submission for the same booking within 24 hours. Community features include threaded comments, likes, favorites, follows, Q&A posts, and reporting/blocking with rate controls (e.g., max 10 posts/hour and 30 comments/hour per account), plus an in-app notification center for replies, mentions, and booking changes. All processing and persistence are local-first: despite referencing Express + Vue.js + MySQL, the delivered application must be fully offline runnable with no independent server, so business logic runs in a frontend service layer over mock data and local JSON configuration, and data is stored in IndexedDB (primary) with LocalStorage for session flags and UI preferences; import/export uses browser File/Blob downloads for backups and JSON restore with schema validation. “Express + MySQL” are treated as development-time stubs only (TypeScript interfaces mirroring expected REST payloads and relational tables) so the app can later be connected without redesign. The system implements a local pseudo-login (username + password) with salted hashing (PBKDF2 via Web Crypto), 5 failed-attempt lockout for 15 minutes, and optional “Remember me” storing only a random session token in LocalStorage. Sensitive fields (health answers, attachments) are encrypted at rest in IndexedDB using AES-GCM with a key derived from the user password; the UI masks sensitive values by default. Real-time status sync is simulated via an in-app event bus and persistent outbox: status changes enqueue messages with idempotency keys, retry up to 5 times with exponential backoff when processing fails, and deduplicate by message hash. A browser-based job scheduler runs every 60 seconds while the app is open to scan due dates (e.g., 24-hour reminders), overdue items, and hold expirations, generating in-app notifications with template variables and frequency limits (no more than 3 notifications per type per day per user). Back-office search indexes bookings, profiles, and posts locally, supporting tokenizer and synonym configuration, incremental updates on save, manual rebuild, and result highlighting with ranking that prioritizes exact title matches over body text.
backend_language: None (Node.js to be integrated in the future)
backend_framework: None (Express.js to be integrated in the future)
